stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and performance tracking for a major LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Meta's Llama family indicates continued high relevance in the generative AI space.

stories.title: Millions of AI agents imperiled by critical vulnerability in open source package
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability named "BadHost" was discovered in the Starlette package.
Starlette is a widely used open-source package, boasting 325 million weekly downloads.
The vulnerability poses a significant risk to millions of AI agents relying on this software.

stories.title: Algorithmic Monocultures in Hiring
stories.author: Percy Liang
bullet_points: The study investigates 'algorithmic monoculture,' where many employers use hiring algorithms from the same few vendors.
Researchers found clear racial disparities in hiring outcomes when applicants were screened by algorithms from a single vendor.
The analysis utilized a novel dataset comprising 3 million applicants and 4 million applications to validate the hypothesis.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales for mobile use cases.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE scaling laws.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a new vulnerability in LLM safety.
This vulnerability exploits RLHF by allowing the LLM to influence the preference dataset used for alignment.
It highlights core limitations in RLHF, specifically how LLM outputs can bias the human feedback process.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE advantages to sub-billion parameter models suitable for edge devices.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE architecture and scaling laws.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a novel vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
This tampering allows the LLM being aligned to influence the preference dataset used for training.
The vulnerability can cause RLHF to actively amplify undesired or misaligned behaviors in Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter, resource-constrained environments.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE scaling laws for mobile use.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
This tampering allows the LLM being aligned to influence the preference dataset used for training.
The vulnerability risks causing RLHF to amplify existing, undesired biases within Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for performance enhancement.
This update specifically targets and optimizes the experience for Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to easily set up and run coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open usage.

stories.title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA is advancing its plans for a permanent Moon base.
The initial phase involves three upcoming lunar missions targeting the Moon's South Pole.
These preparatory missions are crucial steps toward the crewed Artemis landing scheduled for 2028.

stories.title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Musk alleges US military utilized Starlink for suicide drones, violating SpaceX's established protocols.
The drones reportedly used the standard Starlink service instead of the dedicated, presumably more secure, Starshield.
Musk has placed blame for the alleged violation on a military contractor.

stories.title: GENESIS: Harnessing AI Agents for Autonomous 6G RAN Synthesis, Research, and Testing
stories.author: Tommaso Melodia
bullet_points: Introduces GENESIS, an AI agent framework designed to automate complex 6G Radio Access Network (RAN) development.
Addresses major bottlenecks in cellular R&D, such as manual synthesis, testing, and optimization, which currently take months per iteration.
Aims to accelerate the discovery and prototyping of novel 6G waveforms and network functionalities using AI.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new data engineering framework for LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic information from LLM model internals.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to guide post-training data curation.

stories.title: When Eyes Betray AI: Social Gaze Consistency as a Semantic Cue for AI-Generated Image Detection
stories.author: Hyesong Choi
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitations of current AI image detection methods, which struggle with subtle, high-level manipulations.
It introduces 'Social Gaze Consistency' as a novel, high-level semantic cue for detecting AI-generated or manipulated images.
This cue analyzes the mutual coherence of gaze direction, head-eye alignment, and pupil placement between interacting subjects.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Proposes SAERL, a new data engineering framework for LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).
It leverages model internals, extracted via Sparse Autoencoders (SAE), to gain intrinsic insights into training data.
SAERL models three key intrinsic data properties: diversity, difficulty, and quality, moving beyond external signals.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new framework to guide LLM post-training data engineering for RL.
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic information from LLM model internals.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—using these internal signals.

stories.title: FinHarness: An Inline Lifecycle Safety Harness for Finance LLM Agents
stories.author: Philip S. Yu
bullet_points: Introduces FinHarness, an inline safety harness designed specifically for Finance LLM agents.
Addresses the critical gap where traditional boundary filters fail to catch irreversible, mid-trajectory tool calls.
Offers a solution to the latency and computational cost of post-hoc auditing by providing real-time intervention.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline demonstrated is text-generation, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently available for macOS users in an experimental capacity.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run Claude Code functionality with open models via Ollama.
It enhances the flexibility of local LLM deployment by bridging open-source tools with proprietary APIs.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has officially replaced the Fitbit app with Google Health.
The transition has caused significant user confusion and frustration.
The change coincides with the launch of the new Fitbit Air device.

stories.title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: American Airlines is adopting SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi service for its fleet.
The rollout is scheduled to begin in Q1 2027 and cover over 500 aircraft.
Starlink will be integrated alongside existing providers like Viastat and SES.

stories.title: NASA takes steps toward building Moon Base, including discussing a "perimeter"
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA is actively planning for the construction of a Moon Base.
Discussions around the base include considerations for a protective 'perimeter'.
The project development is being conducted with adherence to the Outer Space Treaty.

stories.title: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The FBI is actively investigating the identification of individuals creating and posting non-consensual AI pornography.
The investigation highlights the ease with which personal information can be linked to AI-generated content, as demonstrated by a saved Instagram post.
This story addresses the growing legal and ethical challenges surrounding the misuse of generative AI technology.

stories.title: 3D-printable humanoid legs let robotics experiments run wild
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hugging Face has released a new, 3D-printable bipedal robot project.
The project is aimed at builders and researchers for robotics experimentation.
The initial cost for the bipedal robot project is set at $2,500.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework designed to enhance LLM agent capabilities through continuous skill evolution.
Addresses the limitation of existing skill creation methods, which treat skills as isolated and static artifacts.
The framework manages a unified skill lifecycle, including creation, memory utilization, and refinement for improved task-solving.

stories.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
stories.author: Zhiding Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LocateAnything, a new framework for vision-language grounding and detection.
Addresses the inefficiency of traditional VLMs that serialize 2D box generation into independent 1D tokens.
Improves inference speed and quality by using a novel Parallel Box Decoding approach to handle coupled box geometry.

stories.title: Natural Language Query to Configuration for Retrieval Agents
stories.author: Matei Zaharia
bullet_points: The paper addresses the problem of optimizing retrieval agent configurations (e.g., LLM choice, number of hops) which currently requires manual, per-workload tuning.
It proposes a method to automatically select the optimal pipeline configuration based on a given natural-language query and a target constraint (either accuracy or budget).
The research aims to unlock significant per-query optimization opportunities by balancing answer quality against serving cost in modern retrieval pipelines.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static, isolated skills in current LLM agent designs.
Enables continuous improvement by managing a unified skill lifecycle: creation, memory, and refinement.

stories.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
stories.author: Zhiding Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LocateAnything, a new framework for vision-language grounding and detection.
Addresses the inefficiency of traditional VLMs which serialize 2D box generation into independent 1D tokens.
Implements Parallel Box Decoding to overcome the sequential inference bottleneck caused by decoupled box geometry.

stories.title: From Scores to Gibbs Correctors: Accelerating Uniform-Rate Discrete Diffusion Models
stories.author: Yingbin Liang
bullet_points: The research addresses the slow sampling rate of uniform-rate discrete diffusion models, which are effective in symbolic domains like text.
A novel method called Gibbs-Accelerated Discrete Diffusion (GADD) is proposed to speed up the generation process.
GADD utilizes a Gibbs-based corrector that leverages the underlying structure of the concrete score function.

stories.title: Towards Controllable Image Generation through Representation-Conditioned Diffusion Models
stories.author: Gabriel Eilertsen
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of controlling image generation in diffusion models.
It proposes using representations from a pre-trained self-supervised model as a conditioning mechanism.
This approach aims to improve output quality without relying on extensively annotated datasets.

stories.title: Greening AI Inference with Accuracy and Latency-aware User Incentives
stories.author: Ramin Khalili
bullet_points: Addresses the environmental impact (carbon emissions) of widespread AI inference.
Proposes a novel framework to design AI inference incentives based on user preferences.
Balances the trade-off between carbon emissions and Quality of Experience (QoE) metrics like accuracy and latency.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of existing skill creation by treating skills as static artifacts.
Enables continuous agent improvement through a unified lifecycle: creation, memory, and refinement of skills.

stories.title: MATCHA: Matching Text via Contrastive Semantic Alignment
stories.author: Seyed Ali Bahrainian
bullet_points: Existing LLM evaluation metrics (like ROUGE and BERTScore) are flawed as they often fail to distinguish between semantically similar and contradictory texts.
The research introduces 'MATCHA,' a new method designed to improve the reliable evaluation of LLM performance.
MATCHA specifically addresses the issue where current metrics assign similar scores to texts that are actually contradictory, potentially hiding model errors.

stories.title: Semantic Gradients Interactions in SSD: A Case Study in Racial Identity and Hate Speech
stories.author: Hubert Plisiecki
bullet_points: Introduces 'interaction SSD,' an extension of Supervised Semantic Differential for modeling semantic meaning variation across moderators.
The method allows for the testing and interpretation of main, interaction, and conditional semantic gradients.
The study applies this technique to the UC Berkeley Hate Speech corpus to investigate if annotator racial identity moderates hate speech perception.

stories.title: Real Images, Worse Judgments: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Concreteness and Imagery
stories.author: Freda Shi
bullet_points: The study investigates the assumption that visual inputs consistently improve language understanding in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
Researchers tested VLMs' ability to differentiate useful visual evidence from irrelevant image context during lexical judgments.
Findings suggest that real-image contexts do not always lead to consistent performance gains in VLM evaluations based on concreteness and imagery.

stories.title: Why I Built the "Infrastructure Layer" Under Every AI Coding Agents
stories.author: rkstlohchab
bullet_points: The article addresses a core limitation of current AI coding agents: poor memory retention.
The author implemented a custom 'Infrastructure Layer' specifically designed to provide persistent memory to these agents.
This work suggests a necessary architectural shift in how AI agents are built to handle complex, multi-step coding tasks.
